title: French PM Lecornu warns of 'fairly severe' wildfires arriving unusually early
subhed: According to the prime minister, the wildfire season is running at least two weeks ahead of its usual schedule. With vegetation already extremely dry, he voiced concerns about the 'endurance' of firefighting resources.

title: Heat dome roasts eastern US ahead of Fourth of July celebrations

Germany seeks to introduce a dose of Nordic-style 'flexicurity' into its labor law
subhed: Through a series of reform plans, Berlin has committed to easing dismissal rules alongside measures to support job transitions, aiming to reduce the cost of failure for young companies.

title: 250 years of American independence: 'Enslaved and freed Black people were not merely witnesses to the Atlantic Revolutions, they were active participants'
subhed: Elisabeth Landi, a history professor, argues in this op-ed for Le Monde that commemorations marking the birth of the United States should fully acknowledge the memory of Black fighters who shed their blood for a revolution that did not challenge the institution of slavery.

title: Christopher Nolan's 'The Odyssey' sparks a Homeric debate in Greece
subhed: The casting of a Black actress as Helen of Troy has ignited fierce controversy in Greece. Some lament what they see as Hollywood's appropriation of Greek mythology, while others emphasize the universal nature of these ancient stories.

title: Anthropic President Daniela Amodei: 'We must embrace both the positive side and the dark side of AI'
subhed: The president of the American artificial intelligence start-up, valued at $965 billion and known for its warnings about the risks of this technology, shares her perspective with Le Monde on the major issues facing the sector.

A new radical left is shaking up the Democratic party
subhed: A new generation of candidates, critical of elites, social injustice and US support for Israel, is making its mark in the Democratic primaries ahead of November's midterm elections.

title: Lebanon's Shiites, torn over Hezbollah, question the meaning of their sacrifices
subhed: Days after Lebanon and Israel signed a framework agreement, supporters and opponents of the militia-party are still measuring the consequences of the devastating war.

title: From 'meat grinder' to 'ransom': The key words that define 'Russian soldiers' daily life
subhed: Anthropologist Alexandra Arkhipova has compiled an 'ethnographic dictionary of war' after analyzing some 7,000 letters retrieved from the website of the Russian human rights commissioner. Published in Russian on the independent exile media site 'Echo,' her work reveals the language and suffering of military life.
